challenge_points.description: Claim that Bretton Woods was purely structural and not geopolitical understates the role of Vietnam War financing and Franco-American tensions in triggering the 1971 collapse.
Gold leasing rates and collateral use in derivatives markets can force sales during liquidity squeezes, independent of dollar strength.
Gold price drops during stress are better framed as responses to subsequent monetary policy tightening rather than the geopolitical events themselves.
Both key non-Bretton Woods sources are single opinion/analysis pieces from investing.com and edge-forex.com with no disclosed methodology or sample periods.

first_pass_summary:
# Research Synthesis: Gold-Geopolitics

## 1. WHAT THE RESEARCH FOUND — Honest Synthesis
